title: Versioned Migrations, Expand-Contract Changes, and Rollback Evidence · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds rehearsed schema and data migration chain from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of rehearsed schema and data migration chain while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Keep ordered immutable migration files, checksums, ownership, lock behavior, and applied-state inspection under revision control.
Decide transaction boundaries from each PostgreSQL operation and separate concurrent index, long data, and lock-sensitive work.
Stage additive schema, backfill, dual-read or dual-write, validation, cutover, cleanup, and old-version compatibility.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Migration Ledger
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 2906 during a cross-tenant privacy audit. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove immutable migrations, expand-contract compatibility, bounded backfill, and recovery; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Migration Ledger.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for keep ordered immutable migration files, checksums, ownership, lock behavior, and applied-state inspection under revision control. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Migration Ledger, keep Feed Go Migration Ledger connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

State the Feed Go Migration Ledger preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
